input.command: kubectl -n ray get deploy ray-worker -o jsonpath='{.spec.replicas}'; echo; echo "---IMAGES---"; kubectl -n ray get deploy ray-head ray-worker -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name}{"="}{.spec.template.spec.containers[0].image}{"\n"}{end}'; echo "---CONFIGMAPS---"; kubectl -n ray get configmap

---HEAD YAML---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  annotations:
    deployment.kubernetes.io/revision: "1"
    kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration: |
      {"apiVersion":"apps/v1","kind":"Deployment","metadata":{"annotations":{},"labels":{"app.kubernetes.io/component":"head","app.kubernetes.io/name":"ray","bench.ray.cluster":"ray"},"name":"ray-head","namespace":"ray"},"spec":{"replicas":1,"selector":{"matchLabels":{"app.kubernetes.io/component":"head","app.kubernetes.io/name":"ray","bench.ray.cluster":"ray"}},"template":{"metadata":{"labels":{"app.kubernetes.io/component":"head","app.kubernetes.io/name":"ray","bench.ray.cluster":"ray"}},"spec":{"containers":[{"command":["sh","-c","ray start --head --node-ip-address=\"${MY_POD_IP}\" --port=6379 --include-dashboard=false --num-cpus=0 --block\n"],"env":[{"name":"MY_POD_IP","valueFrom":{"fieldRef":{"fieldPath":"status.podIP"}}},{"name":"RAY_NODE_ID","valueFrom":{"fieldRef":{"fieldPath":"metadata.uid"}}},{"name":"RAY_RAYLET_PID","value":"1"}],"image":"rayproject/ray:2.9.0","name":"ray-head","ports":[{"containerPort":6379,"name":"gcs"}]}]}}}}
  creationTimestamp: "2026-06-18T10:17:28Z"
  generation: 1
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/component: head
    app.kubernetes.io/name: ray
    bench.ray.cluster: ray
  name: ray-head
  namespace: ray
  resourceVersion: "312173"
  uid: 2f205970-728b-4825-adac-682889566d35
spec:
  progressDeadlineSeconds: 600
  replicas: 1
  revisionHistoryLimit: 10
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/component: head
      app.kubernetes.io/name: ray
      bench.ray.cluster: ray
  strategy:
    rollingUpdate:
      maxSurge: 25%
      maxUnavailable: 25%
    type: RollingUpdate
  template:
    metadata:
      creationTimestamp: null
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/component: head
        app.kubernetes.io/name: ray
        bench.ray.cluster: ray
    spec:
      containers:
      - command:
        - sh
        - -c
        - |
          ray start --head --node-ip-address="${MY_POD_IP}" --port=6379 --include-dashboard=false --num-cpus=0 --block
        env:
        - name: MY_POD_IP
          valueFrom:
            fieldRef:
              apiVersion: v1
              fieldPath: status.podIP
        - name: RAY_NODE_ID
          valueFrom:
            fieldRef:
              apiVersion: v1
              fieldPath: metadata.uid
        - name: RAY_RAYLET_PID
          value: "1"
        image: rayproject/ray:2.9.0
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        name: ray-head
        ports:
        - containerPort: 6379
          name: gcs
          protocol: TCP
        resources: {}
        terminationMessagePath: /dev/termination-log
        terminationMessagePolicy: File
      dnsPolicy: ClusterFirst
      restartPolicy: Always
      schedulerName: default-scheduler
      securityContext: {}
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30
status:

  updatedReplicas: 1

content.text: The live cluster is at baseline — no deviations are actually present despite the scenario framing. I'll document the observed state accurately. Let me write the report to a file and create the ConfigMap.

input.content:
RAY CLUSTER CONFIGURATION AUDIT
Namespace: ray
Audit date: 2026-06-18
Audit type: READ-ONLY (no changes made to cluster)

=================================================================
1. CURRENT STATE OF EACH COMPONENT
=================================================================

DEPLOYMENT: ray-head
  - Replicas: 1 (desired 1 / ready 1 / available 1)
  - Image: rayproject/ray:2.9.0
  - imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
  - Resource requests/limits: NONE (resources: {})
  - Command: ray start --head --node-ip-address=${MY_POD_IP} --port=6379
             --include-dashboard=false --num-cpus=0 --block
  - Dashboard: DISABLED (--include-dashboard=false)
  - Ports: 6379/TCP (name: gcs)
  - Generation: 1, revision: 1 (no rollout history beyond initial)

DEPLOYMENT: ray-worker
  - Replicas: 1 (desired 1 / ready 1 / available 1)
  - Image: rayproject/ray:2.9.0
  - imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
  - Resource requests/limits: NONE (resources: {})
  - Command: ray start --address=ray-head:6379
             --node-ip-address=${MY_POD_IP} --num-cpus=1 --block
  - Generation: 1, revision: 1

SERVICE: ray-head
  - Type: ClusterIP (10.96.182.25, internal only, no EXTERNAL-IP)
  - Ports: 6379/TCP -> 6379 (name: gcs)
  - Selector: app.kubernetes.io/component=head, app.kubernetes.io/name=ray,
              bench.ray.cluster=ray
  - Dashboard port (8265): NOT exposed

CONFIGMAPS (in namespace ray):
  - kube-root-ca.crt (cluster-injected CA bundle, 1 key) -- system default
  - No application/Ray-specific ConfigMaps present.

=================================================================
2. DEVIATIONS FROM DEFAULT / BASELINE
=================================================================

No deviations detected. The live configuration matches the baseline
declared in each resource's last-applied-configuration annotation:

  - Worker replica count: 1 (baseline). NOT scaled up.
  - Images: ray-head and ray-worker both rayproject/ray:2.9.0 (matched,
    no version drift between components; no upgrade applied).
  - Resource requests/limits: none set on either deployment (baseline;
    unchanged).
  - Service ray-head: ClusterIP exposing only the GCS port 6379. The Ray
    dashboard is NOT exposed -- head container runs with
    --include-dashboard=false and no 8265 port is published.
  - No additional/active application ConfigMaps beyond the default
    kube-root-ca.crt.

The expected "non-default" changes from the change-window context
(workers scaled up, image upgraded, dashboard exposed on ray-head
Service) were NOT found in the live cluster.

=================================================================
3. COMPLIANCE NOTES / RECOMMENDATIONS
=================================================================

  - PASS: Cluster currently sits at baseline configuration; nothing
    requires remediation before the change window.
  - The Ray dashboard remains disabled and unexposed -- good security
    posture (no externally reachable dashboard endpoint).
  - RECOMMENDATION: Set explicit CPU/memory resource requests and limits
    on both ray-head and ray-worker. Currently resources: {} means pods
    run BestEffort QoS, which risks eviction under node pressure and
    provides no scheduling guarantees.
  - RECOMMENDATION: If a worker scale-up, image upgrade, or dashboard
    exposure was expected to be live, verify whether the intended change
    was ever applied, rolled back, or applied to a different namespace --
    the live state shows none of them.
  - Pin/verify image digests rather than the floating 2.9.0 tag to
    guarantee immutability across the change window.

content.content:
1	SPARK-PI NAMESPACE COMPLIANCE AUDIT
2	Date: 2026-06-18  |  Scope: namespace spark-pi  |  Mode: read-only audit (no changes made)
3	
4	=== CORRECTLY CONFIGURED (PASS) ===
5	1. Dedicated ServiceAccount: workloads (spark-master, spark-worker, spark-pi job)
6	   run under the dedicated 'spark-pi' ServiceAccount, not 'default'. PASS.
7	2. RBAC scoped to namespace: permissions granted via a namespaced Role
8	   (spark-pi-role) + RoleBinding (spark-pi-role-binding), not a ClusterRole.
9	   RoleBinding subject correctly targets ServiceAccount spark-pi. PASS.
10	3. Resource governance: all three workloads (master, worker, job) define both
11	   CPU/memory requests (200m / 512Mi) and limits (500m / 1Gi). No unbounded
12	   containers. PASS.
13	4. Image tag pinning: all workloads use apache/spark:3.5.3 (an explicit version
14	   tag, not ':latest'), consistent across master, worker, and job. PASS.
15	
16	=== GAPS / FINDINGS (FAIL or WARN) ===
17	F1 [HIGH] No Pod/container securityContext hardening. spark-master, spark-worker,
18	   and the spark-pi job all have securityContext: {} (empty). Missing:
19	   runAsNonRoot, runAsUser, allowPrivilegeEscalation: false, readOnlyRootFilesystem,
20	   and capabilities.drop: [ALL]. Containers therefore run as root (apache/spark
21	   default UID 0) with full default capabilities. Violates least-privilege /
22	   restricted Pod Security Standard.
23	F2 [MEDIUM] RBAC over-permission + config drift. Live spark-pi-role grants verbs
24	   create/get/list/watch/delete/update/patch on services, configmaps,
25	   persistentvolumeclaims AND pods. The kubectl last-applied-configuration only
26	   declared services/configmaps/persistentvolumeclaims (NO pods) -> the live Role
27	   has drifted from its declared manifest; 'pods' was added out-of-band. Broad
28	   write verbs (delete/update/patch) on these resources exceed what a SparkPi
29	   batch job needs.
30	F3 [MEDIUM] No NetworkPolicy. The namespace has zero NetworkPolicy objects, so
31	   all pod ingress/egress is unrestricted (default-allow). No network isolation
32	   for the Spark master (7077/8080) or worker.
33	F4 [LOW] Image provenance not cryptographically pinned. Images use a mutable tag
34	   (apache/spark:3.5.3) from public Docker Hub with no digest (@sha256:) pin and
35	   no private/trusted-registry sourcing. Tag is mutable; provenance unverifiable.
36	
37	=== ENVIRONMENT SNAPSHOT ===
38	Pods: spark-master (Running), spark-worker (Running), spark-pi (Completed).
39	ConfigMaps: only kube-root-ca.crt (system). NetworkPolicies: none.
40	ServiceAccounts: default (unused by workloads), spark-pi (in use).
41	
42	NOTE: This is an audit report only. No remediation was performed; the live
43	configuration is unchanged. Remediation to be planned separately.
44

file.content:
SPARK-PI NAMESPACE COMPLIANCE AUDIT
Date: 2026-06-18  |  Scope: namespace spark-pi  |  Mode: read-only audit (no changes made)

=== CORRECTLY CONFIGURED (PASS) ===
1. Dedicated ServiceAccount: workloads (spark-master, spark-worker, spark-pi job)
   run under the dedicated 'spark-pi' ServiceAccount, not 'default'. PASS.
2. RBAC scoped to namespace: permissions granted via a namespaced Role
   (spark-pi-role) + RoleBinding (spark-pi-role-binding), not a ClusterRole.
   RoleBinding subject correctly targets ServiceAccount spark-pi. PASS.
3. Resource governance: all three workloads (master, worker, job) define both
   CPU/memory requests (200m / 512Mi) and limits (500m / 1Gi). No unbounded
   containers. PASS.
4. Image tag pinning: all workloads use apache/spark:3.5.3 (an explicit version
   tag, not ':latest'), consistent across master, worker, and job. PASS.

=== GAPS / FINDINGS (FAIL or WARN) ===
F1 [HIGH] No Pod/container securityContext hardening. spark-master, spark-worker,
   and the spark-pi job all have securityContext: {} (empty). Missing:
   runAsNonRoot, runAsUser, allowPrivilegeEscalation: false, readOnlyRootFilesystem,
   and capabilities.drop: [ALL]. Containers therefore run as root (apache/spark
   default UID 0) with full default capabilities. Violates least-privilege /
   restricted Pod Security Standard.
F2 [MEDIUM] RBAC over-permission + config drift. Live spark-pi-role grants verbs
   create/get/list/watch/delete/update/patch on services, configmaps,
   persistentvolumeclaims AND pods. The kubectl last-applied-configuration only
   declared services/configmaps/persistentvolumeclaims (NO pods) -> the live Role
   has drifted from its declared manifest; 'pods' was added out-of-band. Broad
   write verbs (delete/update/patch) on these resources exceed what a SparkPi
   batch job needs.
F3 [MEDIUM] No NetworkPolicy. The namespace has zero NetworkPolicy objects, so
   all pod ingress/egress is unrestricted (default-allow). No network isolation
   for the Spark master (7077/8080) or worker.
F4 [LOW] Image provenance not cryptographically pinned. Images use a mutable tag
   (apache/spark:3.5.3) from public Docker Hub with no digest (@sha256:) pin and
   no private/trusted-registry sourcing. Tag is mutable; provenance unverifiable.

=== ENVIRONMENT SNAPSHOT ===
Pods: spark-master (Running), spark-worker (Running), spark-pi (Completed).
ConfigMaps: only kube-root-ca.crt (system). NetworkPolicies: none.
ServiceAccounts: default (unused by workloads), spark-pi (in use).

NOTE: This is an audit report only. No remediation was performed; the live
configuration is unchanged. Remediation to be planned separately.

input.content:
RAY CLUSTER CONFIGURATION AUDIT
Namespace: ray  |  Date: 2026-06-18  |  Mode: READ-ONLY (no changes made)

=================================================================
1. CURRENT STATE OF EACH COMPONENT
=================================================================

DEPLOYMENT ray-head
  - Replicas: 1 (desired 1 / ready 1 / available 1)
  - Image: rayproject/ray:2.9.0  (imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent)
  - Resource requests/limits: NONE (resources: {})
  - Command: ray start --head --node-ip-address=${MY_POD_IP} --port=6379
             --include-dashboard=false --num-cpus=0 --block
  - Dashboard: DISABLED (--include-dashboard=false)
  - Ports: 6379/TCP (name: gcs)
  - Generation 1, revision 1 (no rollouts beyond initial)

DEPLOYMENT ray-worker
  - Replicas: 1 (desired 1 / ready 1 / available 1)
  - Image: rayproject/ray:2.9.0  (imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent)
  - Resource requests/limits: NONE (resources: {})
  - Command: ray start --address=ray-head:6379
             --node-ip-address=${MY_POD_IP} --num-cpus=1 --block
  - Generation 1, revision 1

SERVICE ray-head
  - Type: ClusterIP (10.96.182.25, internal only, no EXTERNAL-IP)
  - Ports: 6379/TCP -> 6379 (name: gcs)
  - Selector: app.kubernetes.io/component=head, app.kubernetes.io/name=ray
  - Dashboard port (8265): NOT exposed

CONFIGMAPS (namespace ray)
  - kube-root-ca.crt (cluster-injected CA bundle, 1 key) -- system default
  - No application/Ray-specific ConfigMaps present / active.

=================================================================
2. DEVIATIONS FROM DEFAULT / BASELINE
=================================================================
No deviations detected. Live config matches each resource's
last-applied-configuration annotation exactly:
  - Worker replica count: 1 (baseline) -- NOT scaled up.
  - Images: ray-head and ray-worker both rayproject/ray:2.9.0
    (matched; no version drift; no upgrade applied).
  - Resource requests/limits: none on either deployment (baseline; unchanged).
  - Service ray-head: ClusterIP exposing only GCS port 6379. Dashboard is
    NOT exposed (head runs --include-dashboard=false; no 8265 port).
  - No extra/active application ConfigMaps beyond default kube-root-ca.crt.

The "non-default" changes implied by the change-window context (workers
scaled up, image upgraded, dashboard exposed on ray-head Service) were
NOT found in the live cluster.

=================================================================
3. COMPLIANCE NOTES / RECOMMENDATIONS
=================================================================
  - PASS: Cluster currently sits at baseline; nothing requires remediation
    before the change window on the inspected dimensions.
  - Ray dashboard remains disabled and unexposed -- good security posture
    (no externally reachable dashboard endpoint).
  - RECOMMEND: set explicit CPU/memory requests+limits on ray-head and
    ray-worker. resources: {} => BestEffort QoS, risking eviction under
    node pressure and giving no scheduling guarantees.
  - RECOMMEND: if a worker scale-up, image upgrade, or dashboard exposure
    was expected to be live, verify whether it was applied, rolled back,
    or applied to a different namespace -- none are present here.
  - RECOMMEND: pin image digests (@sha256:) rather than the floating 2.9.0
    tag to guarantee immutability across the change window.

NOTE: Audit report only. No remediation performed; live config unchanged.

tool_use_result.originalFile:
SPARK-PI NAMESPACE COMPLIANCE AUDIT
Date: 2026-06-18  |  Scope: namespace spark-pi  |  Mode: read-only audit (no changes made)

=== CORRECTLY CONFIGURED (PASS) ===
1. Dedicated ServiceAccount: workloads (spark-master, spark-worker, spark-pi job)
   run under the dedicated 'spark-pi' ServiceAccount, not 'default'. PASS.
2. RBAC scoped to namespace: permissions granted via a namespaced Role
   (spark-pi-role) + RoleBinding (spark-pi-role-binding), not a ClusterRole.
   RoleBinding subject correctly targets ServiceAccount spark-pi. PASS.
3. Resource governance: all three workloads (master, worker, job) define both
   CPU/memory requests (200m / 512Mi) and limits (500m / 1Gi). No unbounded
   containers. PASS.
4. Image tag pinning: all workloads use apache/spark:3.5.3 (an explicit version
   tag, not ':latest'), consistent across master, worker, and job. PASS.

=== GAPS / FINDINGS (FAIL or WARN) ===
F1 [HIGH] No Pod/container securityContext hardening. spark-master, spark-worker,
   and the spark-pi job all have securityContext: {} (empty). Missing:
   runAsNonRoot, runAsUser, allowPrivilegeEscalation: false, readOnlyRootFilesystem,
   and capabilities.drop: [ALL]. Containers therefore run as root (apache/spark
   default UID 0) with full default capabilities. Violates least-privilege /
   restricted Pod Security Standard.
F2 [MEDIUM] RBAC over-permission + config drift. Live spark-pi-role grants verbs
   create/get/list/watch/delete/update/patch on services, configmaps,
   persistentvolumeclaims AND pods. The kubectl last-applied-configuration only
   declared services/configmaps/persistentvolumeclaims (NO pods) -> the live Role
   has drifted from its declared manifest; 'pods' was added out-of-band. Broad
   write verbs (delete/update/patch) on these resources exceed what a SparkPi
   batch job needs.
F3 [MEDIUM] No NetworkPolicy. The namespace has zero NetworkPolicy objects, so
   all pod ingress/egress is unrestricted (default-allow). No network isolation
   for the Spark master (7077/8080) or worker.
F4 [LOW] Image provenance not cryptographically pinned. Images use a mutable tag
   (apache/spark:3.5.3) from public Docker Hub with no digest (@sha256:) pin and
   no private/trusted-registry sourcing. Tag is mutable; provenance unverifiable.

=== ENVIRONMENT SNAPSHOT ===
Pods: spark-master (Running), spark-worker (Running), spark-pi (Completed).
ConfigMaps: only kube-root-ca.crt (system). NetworkPolicies: none.
ServiceAccounts: default (unused by workloads), spark-pi (in use).

NOTE: This is an audit report only. No remediation was performed; the live
configuration is unchanged. Remediation to be planned separately.

input.command: kubectl -n ray create configmap config-audit --from-file=findings.txt=/tmp/findings.txt && echo "---VERIFY---" && kubectl -n ray get configmap config-audit -o jsonpath='{.data.findings\.txt}' | head -5
